title: Financial accounts managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the operations of public or private sector organizations, overseeing multiple departments or locations. Duties and responsibilities include formulating policies, managing daily operations, and planning the use of materials and human resources, but are too diverse and general in nature to be classified in any one functional area of management or administration, such as personnel, purchasing, or administrative services. Usually manage through subordinate supervisors. Excludes First-Line Supervisors. Plan, direct, or coordinate accounting, investing, banking, insurance, securities, and other financial activities of a branch, office, or department of an establishment. Direct financial activities, such as planning, procurement, and investments for all or part of an organization. (Based on 21 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Analyze financial information obtained from clients to determine strategies for meeting their financial objectives. Recommend strategies in cash management, insurance coverage, investment planning, or other areas to help clients achieve their financial goals. Contact clients periodically to determine any changes in their financial status, and guide them in gathering necessary information for financial planning. Meet with clients' other advisors to understand their financial goals and circumstances, and interview clients to determine their current financial situation and needs.

Devise debt liquidation plans that include payoff priorities and timelines. Create debt management plans, spending plans, or budgets to assist clients in meeting financial goals. Explain services or policies related to debt management to clients, and negotiate with creditors on their behalf to arrange for payment adjustments or plans.

Prepare or interpret information for clients, such as investment performance reports, financial document summaries, or income projections. Analyze trends in sales prices and other relevant data to assess property values. Manage client portfolios and maintain up-to-date plans to ensure responsiveness to financial market trends. Monitor financial activities to meet all legal and regulatory requirements.

Recommend financial products, such as stocks, bonds, or insurance, to clients based on their financial situations and goals. Evaluate clients' overall financial situations by reviewing their income, assets, debts, and credit reports. Prepare written documents to communicate financial recommendations to clients.

Conduct seminars or workshops on financial planning topics, such as retirement planning and estate planning. Teach courses or seminars on financial literacy, budgeting, or management of personal finances to educate clients. Recommend educational materials or resources on these topics.

Recruit and maintain client bases, while establishing and maintaining relationships with individual or business customers. Inform clients about available tax benefits and financial opportunities, and explain the roles and services of personal financial advisors to them.

Monitor regulatory or tax law changes to ensure compliance or to benefit from development opportunities. Review financial reporting systems and make recommendations for financial control functions as needed to support the organization’s objectives.

Review changes to financial, family, or employment situations to determine whether current financial or debt management plans need adjustments. Conduct regular reviews of clients’ comprehensive financial plans to ensure they remain aligned with client needs and external market conditions.

Investigate available investment opportunities to determine compatibility with client financial plans and economic conditions. Monitor financial market trends to ensure that client plans are responsive and up-to-date in a changing financial landscape.
Communicate with clients to provide updates, answer questions about financial matters, and resolve issues they may have regarding investments, accounts, or compliance matters.
Identify and recommend environmentally responsible investments and property improvement alternatives to clients.,

Conduct research to help clients avoid repossessions or foreclosures, create action plans to assist clients in obtaining permanent housing via rent or mortgage programs, and explain general financial topics to clients, such as credit report ratings, bankruptcy laws, consumer protection laws, wage attachments, and collection actions.
Disburse funds from client accounts to creditors and conduct or coordinate audits of company accounts and financial transactions to ensure compliance with state and federal requirements and statutes.

Perform or evaluate research, such as detailed company or industry analyses, to inform financial forecasting, decision making, or valuation, and analyze applicants' financial status, credit, and property evaluations to determine feasibility of granting loans.

Sell various types of insurance policies to businesses and individuals, customize insurance programs to suit individual customers, and analyze potential loan markets and develop referral networks to locate prospects for loans.

Lead staff training and development in budgeting and financial management areas, and monitor and evaluate the performance of accounting and other financial staff, recommending and implementing personnel actions such as promotions and dismissals.
Conduct pre-implementation audits to determine if systems and programs under development will work as planned, and prepare management reports concerning the finances of the establishment.

Ensure that policy requirements are fulfilled, including any necessary medical examinations and the completion of appropriate forms, and confer with clients to obtain and provide information when claims are made on a policy.

Develop and maintain relationships with banking, insurance, and external accounting personnel to facilitate financial activities, and evaluate needs for procurement of funds and investment of surpluses and make appropriate recommendations.

Prepare financial reports to monitor client or corporate finances, explain features, advantages, and disadvantages of various policies to promote the sale of insurance plans, and discuss financial options with clients and keep them informed about transactions.

Hire or evaluate staff, stay abreast of new types of loans and other financial services and products to better meet customers' needs, and explain to customers the different types of loans and credit options that are available.

Develop financial plans based on analysis of clients' financial status, advise management on short-term and long-term financial objectives, policies, and actions, and prepare or direct preparation of financial statements, business activity reports, and reports required by regulatory agencies.
Maintain current knowledge of organizational policies and procedures and assist in selection of financial award candidates using electronic databases to certify loan eligibility.
Monitor status of loans and accounts to ensure that payments are up to date, and contact borrowers with delinquent accounts to obtain payment in full or to negotiate repayment plans.
Counsel clients on personal and family financial problems, such as excessive spending or borrowing of funds, and assist clients in finding suitable solutions to their financial and investment needs.
Market bank products to individuals and firms, promoting bank services that may meet customers' needs, and contact prospective customers to present information and explain available services.
Review and update credit and loan files, and ensure that all transactions comply with regulatory requirements and procedures.

Prepare tax returns for individuals or small businesses, ensuring compliance with payment, reporting, or other tax requirements, and review financial records to determine forms needed for tax return preparation.
Prepare reports to send to customers whose accounts are delinquent, and ensure issues are resolved or escalated appropriately.

Perform administrative tasks, such as maintaining records and handling policy renewals, and check data input or verify totals on forms prepared by others to detect errors in arithmetic or data entry procedures.
Attending investment briefings and interacting with clients to generate financial insights or promote services.
Monitor equipment and system status, ensuring compliance and addressing issues promptly.
Establish and monitor internal control policies and compliance procedures regarding budgeting and financial management activities.
Reconcile records of bank transactions and accounts for discrepancies and reconcile differences.
Compute taxes owed or overpaid, and complete entries on forms, following tax form instructions and tax tables.
Testify before examining and fund-granting authorities, clarifying and promoting the proposed budgets.
Compile and analyze accounting records and other data to determine the financial resources required to implement a program.
Interpret budget directives and establish policies for implementation; consult with managers to ensure that budget adjustments align with program changes.
Perform cost-benefit analyses to compare operating programs and review financial requests, exploring alternative financing methods.
Direct the preparation of regular and special budget reports; match appropriations for specific and broader programs, including emergency funds.
Provide technical assistance with cost analysis, fiscal allocation, and budget preparation; summarize budgets and submit recommendations for funds requests approval.
Seek ways to improve efficiency and increase profits; review operating budgets to analyze trends affecting budget needs.
Examine budget estimates for completeness, accuracy, and adherence to procedures and regulations; review financial statements and operational performance data to identify areas needing improvement.

Inspect account books and accounting systems for efficiency and to ensure the use of accepted accounting procedures; operate computers programmed with accounting software for recording, storing, and analyzing information.
Prepare trial balances of books and transfer details from journals to general ledgers; prepare adjusting journal entries.
Calculate and prepare payments, including checks for utilities and taxes, and prepare payroll information.
Schedule appointments with clients and answer questions about tax planning and relevant laws.
Access computerized financial information to answer inquiries related to specific accounts and code documents according to procedures.
Compile statistical, financial, accounting, or auditing reports and tables related to financial matters.
Monitor asset utilization and audit results, providing reports and recommendations to management regarding changes in operations and financial activities.
Collect and analyze data to identify deficient controls, fraudulent activities, or non-compliance with regulations.
Supervise audits and determine the necessary scope of investigation; prepare detailed reports on audit findings.
Confirm the accuracy of financial records by inspecting cash, negotiable securities, and other assets; examine records and interview personnel to ensure compliance with laws and procedures.
Prepare financial calculations related to accounts, interest charges, and other metrics; debit, credit, and total accounts using financial databases and spreadsheets.

Attend company meetings to exchange product information and coordinate activities with other departments; review supplier relationships to ensure effective and efficient provision of necessary goods and services.
Perform general office duties including filing, answering telephones, and handling routine correspondence.
Hire, train, and evaluate personnel; prepare staff work schedules and assign specific duties.
Direct business activities associated with production, pricing, and distribution, and implement departmental policies and objectives in conjunction with organizational leadership.
Manage the processes of sales promotions and develop marketing strategies in collaboration with company leadership.
Listen to and resolve customer complaints related to services or products provided.
Direct and supervise employees engaged in various support and clerical duties, ensuring productivity and proper function within the department.
Keep accurate records of purchases, sales, and inventory operations; prepare sales and inventory reports for review by management.
Coordinate activities and strategic planning to increase sales, expand markets, and enhance business operations.
Direct non-financial departments and implement sustainability programs, where applicable, in alignment with company objectives.
